# In Weeks — https://inweeks.org

## What is this?
In Weeks is a free, open-source web tool that visualizes a human life as a grid of approximately 4,000 weeks. It helps people confront the finite nature of time by showing how many weeks they've lived and how many remain.

## How it works
1. Enter your birthday
2. Select your country (life expectancy data for 244 countries/territories from UN World Population Prospects)
3. Optionally select gender for more specific life expectancy
4. See your life as an interactive grid of week squares — filled for lived weeks, empty for remaining
5. Add "life chapters" (school, career, travel, relationships, etc.) with colors
6. Generate and share a portrait image of your life grid

## Key features
- Interactive canvas-rendered grid of ~4,000 weeks
- Life expectancy data for 244 countries and territories
- Colored life chapters with 10 preset colors
- Animated grid reveal
- Shareable story-size image (1080x1920)
- Dark mode
- 100% client-side — no server, no tracking, no cookies, no account
- Data stored only in browser localStorage

## Technical details
- Built with React 19, TypeScript, Vite
- Canvas-based grid rendering for performance
- html-to-image for share card generation
- Zero backend dependencies
- ~84KB gzipped total

## Links
- Website: https://inweeks.org
- Source code: https://github.com/zeck00/inweeks
- Author: Ziad (https://ziad.us)

## Inspiration
Inspired by Tim Urban's "Your Life in Weeks" from Wait But Why, which visualizes a 90-year life as a grid of weeks to illustrate how finite and precious time is.
